Here’s another example. I don’t know how to code, but I used AI to make a bathroom pass app. I explained to ChatGPT that I wanted a pass system in which a student scanned a code and received an email pass, and that I needed a spreadsheet at the end of the day that told me when and where students had gone. I asked ChatGPT to write it for Google’s Apps Script, so I was able to create the app without any conceptual knowledge of what I was doing.

This does raise some issues concerning accuracy and especially long-term maintenance. I am trying to be more intentional about what AI generates. I appreciate that AI lets me build things I couldn’t have otherwise; I just want to be thoughtful about how I use it.

record.text: Ezért kell olyan AI eszközt használni youtube videókra, amely megjeleníti a teljes átiratot is, azt te magad is át tudod pörgetni. Néhány videónál magán a youtube oldalon is lehet látni az Átirat megjelenítése gombot és akkor ott AI nélkül is át tudod pörgetni a szöveget, de ritkán látható.

A te idő- és energiaráfordításod max kb 5 perc, amíg a Googlen keresel egy éppen használható összefoglaló oldalt, majd ctrl+c ctrl+v-vel bemásolod a videó linkjét és megnyomod az összefoglalás gombot.

Utána már saját tempódban olvashatod, jóval gyorsabban mint a videó megnézése.

Ez pont egy jó felhasználasa az AI-nak: vannak videó-összefoglaló AIk meg oldalak, amikkel megeteted a videót, majd a youtube vagy a feltöltő által készített feliratozás alapján összefoglalja, meg akár a teljes átiratot is olvashatóvá teszi.

external.description: OpenAI近期发表名为《智能时代产业政策》的白皮书，提出在AI快速迈向“超智能”阶段时，必须从制度上重新分配技术带来的收益。白皮书认为，AI能极大提高生产力、降低成本并加速医学与科学突破，但同时可能造成岗位流失、产业重组和财富集中，因此仅靠市场无法保证多数人受益。为此，OpenAI建议将AI视为类似电力和网络的基础设施，保证个人、企业与学校能负担得起；设立“公共财富基金”共享产业成长收益；探讨对自动化的税制改革，并鼓励企业把效率红利转化为更高薪酬、更好福利或每周四天工时。此外，白皮书主张降低创业门槛以促进小企业兴起、扩大教育与照护等人本产业的就业机会、强化社会安全网以及加大科研与基础设施投入。针对风险治理，文件还提出建立审计与安全测试、可追溯与验证机制，并推动国际间资讯共享以应对资安、生物或模型失控等威胁。不过多家外媒对这些构想抱持怀疑态度，批评机器人税与公共基金在现实操作上难以推动，质疑该机构借政策话语强化自身影响，甚至指出缺乏可强制执行的监管工具。总体来看，OpenAI把讨论从技术能力延伸到分配与治理层面，试图在AI带来巨大红利与风险并存时，提出一套制度性应对方案，但其可行性与执行力仍面临激烈争论。
external.title: 震撼！OpenAI放大招：要全民分AI红利？“4天工周+公共财富基金”引爆争议

この AI ローカル実行ツールの動向は、SYUTAINβ のデータ解析ワークフローと直接関連する可能性があります。

Spent 3h debugging an AI agent. Fix was one line. The hard part of AI automation isn't the tech — it's writing precise instructions for what you actually want.

My AI agent drafted a client proposal while I was on a call.

I sent it without reading it.

It was good. Better than my usual first draft.

That scared me more than if it had been bad.

Most founders use AI like a search engine.

Ask a question. Get an answer. Close the tab.

The unlock is when AI runs *between* your tasks — not just inside them.

That's when solo starts to feel like a team.

Öl für 100 € das Fass ist nicht wettbewerbsfähig mit erneuerbaren Energien erzeugt in Deutschland.

Wer durch Subventionen die Marktpreise jetzt senkt, auf Rechnung aller Steuerzahler, baut die Abhängigkeit von Öl und Gas aus und stützt die Autokraten der Welt: die Mullahs, Putin, Trump.

Er vergibt wieder eine Chance für eine schnellere Dekarbonisierung durch Erneuerbare, produziert in Deutschland. Eine Chance für mehr Energieunabhängigkeit.

Ein Barrel Öl kostet aktuell rund 100 € und enthält etwa 1.600 kWh Energie. Genau diese Energiemenge lässt sich durch Solarstrom in Deutschland heute für rund 50 € erzeugen - also für die Hälfte.

Dafür braucht es lediglich eine Solarfläche von etwa sieben Quadratmetern.

Jahr für Jahr erzeugt diese Fläche dieses „Fass" Energie - ohne weitere Kosten 40 oder 50 Jahre lang oder vielleicht länger. Und trotzdem hält sich bis heute das Märchen, Erneuerbare seien zu teuer.

Vor diesem Hintergrund ist es kaum noch rational, Öl aus Iran, Russland oder anderen weit entfernten Ländern nach Deutschland zu verschiffen. Wer daran festhält, verteidigt kein Erfolgsmodell, sondern eine gefährliche Abhängigkeit.

Erst diese Abhängigkeit gibt den Mullahs die Macht dem iranischen Volk menschenunwürdige Lebensbedingungen zuzumuten und Israel mit der Auslöschung zu drohen.

Nicht nur meine Meinung, wie die Diskussionsrunde bei Markus Lanz mit namhaften deutschen Ökonomen zeigt.

Hier geht's zur Sendung: https://lnkd.in/dwDWwbWq

🇮🇷 1. “The Iranian proposal drew enthusiastic praise from over a hundred world leaders…”
There is no public record of:
- an Iranian proposal receiving praise from “over a hundred world leaders,”
- or any coordinated global endorsement on that scale,
- or any such statement being attributed to Iran in current reporting.
When something does get that level of international support (Paris Agreement, UN resolutions, ceasefire frameworks), it’s widely documented. Nothing like that exists here.
So this part is not supported by any known reporting.

🇫🇷 2. “including France’s Emmanuel Macron…”
There is no record of Macron praising an Iranian proposal in those terms.
Macron has commented on Iran in various contexts — nuclear negotiations, regional stability, hostage diplomacy — but nothing resembling “enthusiastic praise” for an Iranian initiative.
So this part is not accurate.

🇨🇦 3. “and Canada’s Mark Carney…”
This one is the clearest red flag.
- Mark Carney is not Canada’s leader.
He is a former central banker (Bank of Canada, Bank of England) and a public figure, but not a head of government.
- There is no record of him praising an Iranian proposal as “long overdue.”
So this part is incorrect on both identity and content.

🧭 Bottom line
Your quoted sentence is not accurate:
- No evidence of praise from “over a hundred world leaders.”
- No evidence Macron said anything like that.
- Mark Carney is not Canada’s leader and did not say this.
If you want, I can help you rewrite the line so it’s grounded in real diplomatic behavior, or I can check what actual statements world leaders have made about Iran recently.

The Borowitz Report is satire, not straight news.
Andy Borowitz writes for The New Yorker, and his pieces are intentionally absurd, deadpan political humor written in the style of news articles. They’re meant to sound like real reporting but are actually jokes — very much in the tradition of The Onion, just with a more polished, New Yorker tone.
A few quick markers:
- It always runs under the “Humor” or “Satire” section.
- The headlines are deliberately exaggerated or surreal.
- Nothing in a Borowitz piece should be treated as factual reporting.
So if something sounds wild, over‑the‑top, or too perfectly ridiculous to be real — that’s the point.

This course uses artificial intelligence tools in parts of the content creation process, with full instructor review and oversight to ensure quality and accuracy.

Most Django courses fail because they rush through Python. This one doesn't.

If you've tried to learn Django and hit a wall because classes, decorators, or the ORM felt like magic, this course is your solution. We believe you can't master a web framework until you master the language it's built on.

This is a unique, two-part masterclass:

First, we master Python. Not just the basics—we build an unshakable foundation. We explore data types, every essential built-in method, functions, OOP, and key modules with a level of detail you won't find anywhere else.

Then, we apply it all to Django. With your Python fundamentals solid, building the full-stack Vehicle Dealership application becomes a logical, empowering next step—not a confusing leap.

Who This Course Is FOR (And Who It's NOT For):

FOR YOU IF:

You are a beginner or self-taught programmer who values deep understanding over quick hacks. You want to know the "why" behind every line of code.

You've felt frustrated by courses that skip fundamentals. You appreciate patient, thorough explanations (each major concept is broken into dedicated Theory + Practical lectures).

Your goal is long-term competence as a developer, starting with a portfolio-ready Django project.

NOT FOR YOU IF:

You want a "quick Django crash course." We spend significant time on Python fundamentals first.

PART 1: The Python Encyclopedia (The "Why" Behind Django)

Installation & Editor Setup: Detailed guides for PyCharm, VS Code, and plugins.

Deep Dive on Data Structures: Strings, Lists, Dictionaries, Sets, Tuples—exploring their methods in detail.

Core Programming Logic: Conditionals, Loops (While/For), Functions (including Lambda, Recursion).

Object-Oriented Programming (OOP): Classes, Inheritance, Abstract Classes—the bedrock of Django's design.

Essential Modules: Modules, Datetime, JSON, RegEx, and an introduction to NumPy & Pandas.

Visual Learning: We even use Python's Turtle module to reinforce programming logic in a visual, memorable way.

Design database models (Cars, Agents, Users) using Django's ORM.

Deploy your finished, full-stack application live to the web.

No Magic, Just Understanding: You'll never copy-paste code you don't understand. We build knowledge from the ground up.

Theory + Practical for Every Concept: This method ensures comprehension, not just mimicry.

Builds Developer Confidence: By the time you reach Django, you'll have the Python skill to debug, modify, and think beyond the tutorial.

Enroll today if you're ready for the most thorough, comprehensive path from Python beginner to confident Django developer.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Nach einem absurden Busunfall startet für den Büro-Sklaven Arihito Atobe ein neues Leben in einer Fantasywelt. Als sogenannter Seeker landet er in der seltenen Klasse „Nachthut“, die die Party aus der hinteren Reihe mit Angriff, Verteidigung und Heilung unterstützt. Seine Gruppe besteht nur aus Mädchen, und seine Klasse hat einen skurrilen Bonus: Allein hinten zu stehen steigert ihre Zuneigung zu ihm. Arihito nutzt die Chance für einen kompletten Neustart fernab vom Firmenalltag.
